calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-27-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Gary Royce appearing for petitioner
Original receipts of four heirs have been filed and the other two were recieved by Mr. Royce on Saturday. He will file them by tommorow. Mr. Royce states the surcharge for six months to be at 4%. Mr. Royce also states that his extrodinary fees include filings made at Santa Clara, Nevada and S.F. Mr. Royce is to submit a declaration explaining the different cost and which cost applies to which court. He is also to submit an proposed order, leaving it blank, for the court to fill in the amounts. Matters are taken under submission.
COMMISSIONER: DOROTHY MCMATH, REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN, CST. #11722, CLERK:LARRISA DOTSON, BALIFF: SMITH

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-25-2007 Continued To Jul-30-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Gary Royce appearing for petitioner. Fees issue and surcharge interest issue addressed. Court instructed counsel to file a declaration identifying who the beneficiaries are in the other three estate and requested to have extraordinary fees allocated to people who benefited. Matter is continued to 7/30. OSC is discharge.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Tony Santiago.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2007 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-12-2007 Continued To Apr-30-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Royce (Counsel for Lillian Laymon, Personal Representative).

Mr. Royce appeared to request an additional continuance to address the issues of the extraordinary fees and the documentation required for the accounting. Although the Court agreed to continue the matter, it expressed significant concerns with respect to the number of continuances thus far and the procedural issues that remain outstanding and as to how a continuance would resolve those issues. The hearing was continued to April 30, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.
